summary: Implemented ADR 0190 from the latest `origin/main` worktree by adding the synthetic transaction replay harness, wiring it into restore verification, and replaying the restored `docker-runtime-lv3` target from production backups; the governed live path now reaches the guest through Proxmox `qga` when SSH never appears, but the restored docker-runtime services still fail recovery validation, so platform-version advancement remains blocked.

verification.observed: `python3 -m py_compile scripts/restore_verification.py scripts/synthetic_transaction_replay.py` passed, `uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml pytest tests/test_synthetic_transaction_replay.py tests/test_restore_verification.py tests/test_restore_verification_windmill.py -q` passed with `13 passed`, and `uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python scripts/validate_repository_data_models.py --validate` passed after the ADR 0190 workstream surfaces landed.

`make workflow-info WORKFLOW=restore-verification`, `make synthetic-transaction-replay SYNTHETIC_REPLAY_ARGS='--target restore-docker-runtime --dry-run'`, and `make restore-verification RESTORE_ARGS='--help'` all exercised the updated workflow, catalog, and Make entrypoints from this isolated worktree.

The first replay from this worktree wrote `receipts/restore-verifications/2026-03-27-ws0190-attempt1.json`, proving the restore path from current production backups but exposing two unrelated live gaps outside ADR 0190 itself: the default 5-minute SSH wait was too short for restored guests, and `backup-lv3` currently has no PBS backup available.

The focused `docker-runtime-lv3` replay wrote `receipts/restore-verifications/2026-03-27-ws0190-attempt2-qga.json` and showed the new Proxmox guest-agent fallback working end to end: the restored guest never exposed SSH, but the workflow still executed guest-local smoke probes and the full ADR 0190 synthetic replay through `qga`.

The final focused replay in `receipts/restore-verifications/2026-03-27.json` still failed the actual recovery target: Keycloak loopback discovery returned connection refused, NetBox and Windmill reset loopback connections, OpenBao remained sealed with HTTP `503`, and all four synthetic control-plane scenarios reported `0/12` successful requests on the restored target even after a 420-second warm-up window.

notes: This workstream intentionally does not bump `VERSION`, change `changelog.md`, update `README.md`, or advance `versions/stack.yaml` because it is not the protected integration step on `main`.

ADR 0190 repository implementation is complete on this branch, but `Implemented In Platform Version` remains `not yet` because the restored docker-runtime services themselves are still unhealthy during replay.

The most actionable follow-up is not more harness work: it is understanding why the restored docker-runtime backup comes up with OpenBao sealed and the other control-plane services still unavailable on loopback even after extended warm-up.
